purpose: Per-task outcome record for the TASK-HMIG-010 feature-level LangGraph validation run. Populated by the operator after each task completes (the runner does not yet auto-emit this — see TASK-FIX-CANARY-PARSER for the analogous gap on the canary side).

run_attempts.notes: Aborted at Wave 1 / TASK-FIX-IA03 / Turn 1 with LangGraphHarnessError (auth). Root cause: feature subcommand doesn't thread --model. See incident I-001 (F9) in feature-run-incidents.md and TASK-FIX-LGFM.

run_attempts.notes: First run under TASK-HMIG-013 substrate posture (Player=qwen36-workhorse, Coach=gemma4:26b). Architecture invariants from runs 1-7 ALL confirmed working: COACHSF01 (F17 fix) fired exactly as designed on turn 1 — Coach emitted 4898 chars content + 0 reasoning, no fenced JSON → synthetic feedback emitted → Player produced substantive 46-file turn-2 retry. CTOUT01 (F14 fix) fired exactly as designed on turn 2 — Coach hung 990s+, harness.cancel() propagated, task CANCELLED at 3000s boundary. New findings: F20 (gemma4-coach n_ctx=65536 too small, HIGH operator-fixable, I-009 filed), F21 (turn-2 Coach hard-stall downstream of F20, MEDIUM substrate-quality, I-010 filed). AC-009 NOT exercised (--reasoning off; the 4898-char prose was the COACHSF01-handled shape, not the AC-009 surface). Operator-side mitigation: llama-swap n_ctx bump 65536 → 98304 (recommended) or 131072. Re-run 9 with --fresh after llama-swap reload.

Per AC-006 definition ('any failure the operator cannot resolve without code edits to the harness itself'), F20 is RECOVERABLE — operator bumps n_ctx in llama-swap and re-runs. Substrate-sizing fixes do not require code edits.

task_outcome_schema.first_pass_success: true|false (bool, required) — true iff coach_decision == 'approve' AND turns_used == 1
task_outcome_schema.resume_used: true|false (bool, required) — true iff a --resume invocation was needed
task_outcome_schema.non_recoverable: true|false (bool, required) — true iff failure required code edits to the harness itself per AC-006 definition
task_outcome_schema.notes: free-form string, optional — failure analysis, resume rationale, divergence-from-canary commentary
